nodes.title: Anna Lindberg

body.processed:
Anna is the creative force behind Hearthwood's most iconic collections, including Vermont Modern. Trained in Scandinavian furniture design, she brings a deep understanding of form, function, and materiality to every piece she creates.

Her philosophy centers on the idea that great furniture should disappear into daily life -- beautiful enough to admire, comfortable enough to forget. Anna's designs have been featured in Dwell, Architectural Digest, and the Smithsonian Cooper Hewitt Design Museum.

nodes.education: Royal Danish Academy of Fine Arts

body.processed:
Robert is our master craftsman and workshop director, responsible for translating design concepts into production-ready pieces. A fourth-generation woodworker, he learned traditional joinery from his grandfather and has spent three decades perfecting techniques that make Hearthwood furniture structurally superior.

Under Robert's direction, our workshop produces furniture using time-honored methods -- mortise-and-tenon joints, dovetails, and hand-rubbed finishes -- that ensure every piece will last for generations.

nodes.title: Mei Tanaka

body.processed:
Mei leads our interior design consultation service, helping clients select and arrange Hearthwood pieces to transform their living spaces. With a background in architecture and interior design, she has an exceptional eye for proportion, flow, and the interplay between furniture and space.

Mei's room designs have been published in House Beautiful and she regularly speaks at design conferences about sustainable furniture in modern interiors.
